description:
This map displays detailed Land Use / Land Cover (LULC) classifications for an urban and peri-urban area in Turku, Finland, as indicated by an inset map showing Finland with Turku highlighted by a black square on its southwest coast. The main map features a colour-coded legend:
*   Continuous urban fabric (dark red)
*   Discontinuous dense urban fabric (red)
*   Discontinuous medium density urban fabric (medium red)
*   Discontinuous low density urban fabric (light red)
*   Discontinuous very low density urban fabric (very light red)
*   Roads (grey)
*   Pastures (yellow)
*   Green urban areas (light green)
*   Arable land (very light yellow)
*   Forests (dark green)

The map includes two white stars with black outlines as points of interest. Dashed black lines indicate measured distances between points: 315 m from the lower left star, 700 m along a path, and then from the upper right star, 300 m and 200 m along another path. A light blue line represents a water body. The scale bar shows distances up to 0.5 km (0, 0.15, 0.3, 0.5 km). The spatial pattern reveals an intricate mix of various urban fabric densities, green urban areas, forests, pastures, and arable land, typical of a developing urban fringe, with a clear network of roads. This map exemplifies the detailed geographical information provided by the Copernicus Urban Atlas for urban planning and environmental monitoring.
